title: Synsepalum subverticillatum

intro: Synsepalum subverticillatum, also known as the West African berry, is a plant species belonging to the family Sapotaceae, native to tropical Africa. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, offering potential health benefits such as lowering blood pressure, reducing inflammation, and improving digestion. The bioactive constituents of Synsepalum subverticillatum include flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, the plant is often prepared as a tea or infusion, as well as in the form of dried berries, which are used to treat various health conditions, including hypertension and gastrointestinal issues.
